About: It is about gold miners who revolted against the British administration of the colony of Victoria, Australia, during the Victorian gold rush.
Affects: It affects the cost of mining permits and the officious way the system was enforced.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Victorian gold rush.
It is associated with the Battle of the Eureka Stockade.
It is associated with Peter Lalor.
It is associated with the Eureka Flag.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the colony of Victoria, Australia.
CausedBy: It is caused by the cost of mining permits and the officious way the system was enforced.
It is caused by the introduction of a tax on gold mines.
It is caused by the murder of a gold miner and the burning of a local hotel.
HasA: It has grievances concerning the cost of mining permits.
It has a preceding period of peaceful demonstrations and civil disobedience.
It has a de facto leader named Peter Lalor.
It has a legacy that is contested.
It has reforms implemented, including universal adult male suffrage.
It has origins in the Australian gold rush that began in 1851.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the Battle of the Eureka Stockade.
It is characterized by the Eureka Flag.
It is characterized by the grievances of gold miners.
It is characterized by peaceful demonstrations and civil disobedience.
It is characterized by open rebellion.
It is characterized by mass public support leading to acquittal.
It is characterized by the birth of democracy in Australia.
It is characterized by a political revolt.
Influenced: It influenced legislation providing for universal adult male suffrage for Assembly elections.
It influenced the removal of property qualifications for Legislative Assembly members.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the introduction of a tax on gold mines.
It was influenced by the murder of a gold miner and the burning of a local hotel.
It was influenced by the cost of mining permits.
It was influenced by the officious way the system was enforced.
It was influenced by the strict prohibition of liquor imposed by the government.
It was influenced by the need for improvements in the road from Melbourne.
It was influenced by the lack of police protection.
It was influenced by the disquiet on the goldfields.
KnownFor: It is known for the Battle of the Eureka Stockade.
It is known for the Eureka Flag.
It is known for the grievances of gold miners.
It is known for the implementation of reforms.
It is known for its contested legacy.
It is known for being identified with the birth of democracy in Australia.
LocatedIn: It is located in the colony of Victoria, Australia.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Victorian gold rush.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the colony of Victoria, Australia.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Australian gold rush that began in 1851.
It originated in the colony of Victoria, Australia.
PartOf: It is part of the Victorian gold rush.
PerformedBy: It was performed by gold miners.
